# flashblade_certificate (Data Source)

Reads an existing FlashBlade certificate by name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "flashblade_certificate" "example" {
  name = "my-tls-cert"
}

output "certificate_status" {
  value = data.flashblade_certificate.example.status
}

output "certificate_valid_to" {
  value = data.flashblade_certificate.example.valid_to
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the certificate to look up.

### Read-Only

- `certificate` (String) The PEM-encoded X.509 certificate body.
- `certificate_type` (String) The certificate type (e.g. appliance, external).
- `common_name` (String) The common name (CN) extracted from the certificate.
- `country` (String) The country (C) field extracted from the certificate.
- `email` (String) The email address extracted from the certificate.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the certificate.
- `intermediate_certificate` (String) The PEM-encoded intermediate certificate chain.
- `issued_by` (String) The issuer of the certificate.
- `issued_to` (String) The subject of the certificate.
- `key_algorithm` (String) The key algorithm (e.g. RSA, EC).
- `key_size` (Number) The key size in bits.
- `locality` (String) The locality (L) field extracted from the certificate.
- `organization` (String) The organization (O) field extracted from the certificate.
- `organizational_unit` (String) The organizational unit (OU) field extracted from the certificate.
- `state` (String) The state/province (ST) field extracted from the certificate.
- `status` (String) The certificate status (e.g. imported, self-signed).
- `subject_alternative_names` (List of String) The subject alternative names (SANs) extracted from the certificate.
- `valid_from` (Number) The Unix timestamp (milliseconds) from which the certificate is valid.
- `valid_to` (Number) The Unix timestamp (milliseconds) until which the certificate is valid.
